goal: Find one or more new orthogonal specialist signals or conditioning layers that work where active-signal-v1@1 is absent, prove their additive value, and avoid repeating v1's single-filter event-entry mistakes.
global_constraints: Base active-signal-v1@1 is immutable: no retuning of hours, days, thresholds, or execution rules.
Raw single-filter event-entry constructions (H1/H2 style) are permanently banned.
Every new candidate must first pass complement-first mode before any other evaluation.
Model-first approaches are forbidden until the low-friction Wave A is fully exhausted.
Each prototype must have at least three logical layers: new information layer, regime/state confirmation, and entry-density limiter.
A candidate whose profit vanishes under strict-additive mode is rejected regardless of permissive-integrated performance.

slices.title: Cannibalization, ownership and new-entry proof
slices.hypothesis: Formally prove that each surviving candidate contributes genuinely new trades and new profit rather than parasitizing on base entries.
slices.objective: Formally prove that each surviving candidate contributes genuinely new trades and new profit rather than parasitizing on base entries.
success_criteria: Direct proof exists of which candidates genuinely expand the system.
evidence_requirements: Direct proof exists of which candidates genuinely expand the system.

slices.title: Wave B — medium-friction branch (exploration)
slices.hypothesis: Explore cross-symbol leadership, model-backed compression/routing, and event-state-model hybrids only if Wave A failed to produce a conclusive additive candidate.
slices.objective: Explore cross-symbol leadership, model-backed compression/routing, and event-state-model hybrids only if Wave A failed to produce a conclusive additive candidate.
success_criteria: Wave B branches are evaluated with at least one candidate reaching standalone backtest.
evidence_requirements: Wave B branches are evaluated with at least one candidate reaching standalone backtest.

slices.title: Wave B — medium-friction branch (construction)
slices.hypothesis: Explore cross-symbol leadership, model-backed compression/routing, and event-state-model hybrids only if Wave A failed to produce a conclusive additive candidate.
slices.objective: Explore cross-symbol leadership, model-backed compression/routing, and event-state-model hybrids only if Wave A failed to produce a conclusive additive candidate.
success_criteria: Wave B branches are evaluated with at least one candidate reaching standalone backtest.
evidence_requirements: Wave B branches are evaluated with at least one candidate reaching standalone backtest.
